title: Time-lapse of colony development on King's B medium (24–72 h): Pantoea stewartii and associated bacterial communities
author: Matičič, Janja
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This time-lapse video shows the development of a colony on King's B medium over a period of 24–72 hours of incubation. This is relevant to the isolation of Pantoea stewartii and associated bacterial communities. 
 It shows the temporal changes in colony morphology and the increasing complexity of the community during the isolation process from maize and related host plants. While early stages display relatively simple growth patterns, later stages reveal mixed communities comprising morphologically similar colonies from different taxa. 
 The accompanying reference image highlights: top row: Pantoea stewartii isolates (target pathogen); the middle row shows morphologically similar non-target genera (e.g. Sphingomonas, Curtobacterium, Microbacterium and Agrobacterium); bottom row: other Pantoea species (e.g. P. ananatis, P. agglomerans and P. vagans). 
 This resource aids the interpretation of colony morphology and illustrates the limitations of morphology-based identification in complex plant samples. 
 This video complements the poster: 'MALDI-TOF MS profiling of maize isolation plates: from background flora to overlooked pathogens' (EPPO Conference on Diagnostics of Plant Pests – Recent Developments and Future Trends, Vienna, Austria, 22–24 April 2026). 
 YouTube version (for easy viewing):  https://youtu.be/1oECSotA8Ns  
 We acknowledge the contribution of the diagnostic team in generating and interpreting isolation data.
